channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump's name must come off of the Kennedy Center, judge rules
item.description: The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now
item.content:encoded:
added to the Kennedy Center in December of 2025. This photo was taken in April 2026.'/>

The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: President Trump's push to be on a new $250 bill
item.description: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with financial historian Brendan Greeley about President Trump's push to be featured on a new $250 bill.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Sacha Pfeiffer talks with financial historian Brendan Greeley about President Trump's push to be featured on a new $250 bill.
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: Louisiana's crawfish industry disrupted after migrant worker shakeup
item.description: Louisiana's crawfish industry is upended because processing plants can't get guest-worker visas for its seasonal workforce.
item.content:encoded: Louisiana's crawfish industry is upended because processing plants can't get guest-worker visas for its seasonal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: Which first lady feared her husband might be having a stroke? The quiz knows
item.description: This week, the pope took aim at AI, a fancy carmaker dipped a toe in the EV market and a first lady made a surprising comment.
item.content:encoded: This week, the pope took aim at AI, a fancy carmaker dipped a toe in the EV market and a first lady made a surprising comment.
item.dc:creator: Holly J. Morris

item.title: Why the U.S. cattle herd is at a 75-year low — and what it means for beef prices
item.description: The overall number of U.S. beef and dairy cattle has shrunk to its lowest level since 1951. Drought, rising operating costs and increased consolidation are among the causes.
item.content:encoded:
The overall number of U.S. beef and dairy cattle has shrunk to its lowest level since 1951. Drought, rising operating costs and increased consolidation are among the causes.

(Image credit: Luke Sharrett for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Bondi doubles down on her handling of Epstein files in testimony to Congress
item.description: In a closed-door interview, Bondi said there were redaction errors, but otherwise defended the Justice Department's release of files in the Epstein case. She was ousted as attorney general in April.
item.content:encoded:
In a closed-door interview, Bondi said there were redaction errors, but otherwise defended the Justice Department's release of files in the Epstein case. She was ousted as attorney general in April.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: The Corporate Transparency Act once had bipartisan support. What happened?
item.description: The Corporate Transparency Act was supposed to shed light on shell companies. It had bipartisan support, until it didn't. Why does the Trump administration want to shelve it?
item.content:encoded: The Corporate Transparency Act was supposed to shed light on shell companies. It had bipartisan support, until it didn't. Why does the Trump administration want to shelve it?
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: In today's digital age, paper ephemera attracts new generation of collectors
item.description: We have less need for paper than past generations, but paper ephemera is still very collectible. We meet some digital natives who collect old postcards and comics at a paper show in Pennsylvania.
item.content:encoded: We have less need for paper than past generations, but paper ephemera is still very collectible. We meet some digital natives who collect old postcards and comics at a paper show in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Buffy Gorrilla

item.title: How a friendship grew from an agoraphobia diagnosis
item.description: About 10 years ago, Naomi "Nonie" Lynn was diagnosed with agoraphobia or the fear of public places. She sits down with Mandy Lashay to share how Mandy's visits turned into a friendship.
item.content:encoded: About 10 years ago, Naomi "Nonie" Lynn was diagnosed with agoraphobia or the fear of public places. She sits down with Mandy Lashay to share how Mandy's visits turned into a friendship.
item.dc:creator: Jasmyn Morris

item.title: At least 3 are dead after explosion at Dallas apartment complex. Others are missing
item.description: An explosion at an apartment complex in Dallas, Texas, killed at least three people, including a child. Authorities say more victims could be found.
item.content:encoded: An explosion at an apartment complex in Dallas, Texas, killed at least three people, including a child. Authorities say more victims could be found.
item.dc:creator: Dylan Duke
